response.justification: Strong, hands-on engineer with directly relevant exposure to AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflows in the current Staff role, plus genuine high-throughput/distributed-systems experience (micro-services handling millions of requests/second at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby). However, the must-have 'strong Python' is absent from the skills list and employment history (stack is TypeScript/Node, DLang, Java, C#), and evidence of staff-level technical strategy and cross-functional stakeholder leadership is thinner than the IC craftsmanship signal. Worth an interview to probe Python depth and strategy scope.
strengths: Direct AI-native experience: MCP code generators and agentic-workflow tooling
Proven high-throughput distributed systems (millions of req/sec, low-latency)
Deep craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based, broad polyglot range
concerns: No Python evidence despite it being a must-have skill
Staff-level technical strategy and stakeholder leadership under-demonstrated
Breadth-heavy, somewhat scattered profile; limited depth at scale recently
